content.full_text:
December 11, 2000

Charles M. Marsteller
835 Turk, #608
San Francisco, CA 94102

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. W-00-180

Dear Mr. Marsteller:

This letter confirms our recent conversation where you stated that you were withdrawing your August 7, 2000, correspondence to the Fair Political Practices Commission requesting advice. As we discussed, the question of the San Francisco Ethics Commission’s authority to audit state committees that are active in local elections is being explored by the San Francisco Ethics Commission itself, as well as being discussed by the FPPC and the San Francisco Commission. Please note that under the provisions of the Political Reform Act¹, the Commission can only give advice to parties who possess a duty or obligation under the Act, we cannot provide advice to third parties. (Regulation 18329(b)(8)(B).) Should you have any additional questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Luisa Menchaca
Assistant General Counsel

By: Hyla P. Wagner
Senior Counsel, Legal Division

HPW:jg

¹ Government Code sections 81000-91015. Commission regulations appear at title 2, sections 18109-18996, of the California Code of Regulations.
